title: Mental health access is not an abstract systems challenge—it’s a daily reality for communities navigating long wait times, workforce shortages, and fragmented care.
post:
Mental health access is not an abstract systems challenge—it’s a daily reality for communities navigating long wait times, workforce shortages, and fragmented care.

That’s why conversations at #NatCon26 matter.

I’ll be there with McKinsey Health Institute and leaders from across the behavioral health community to discuss not only the growing momentum behind the brain economy, but what it takes to translate that momentum into real access—through scalable models like task sharing and Certified Community Behavioral Health Clinics (CCBHCs). 

In an AI-enabled world, access to technology is no longer the differentiator—human capability is. The strength, agility, and resilience of our brains underpin productivity, innovation, and inclusive growth. But those capabilities depend on systems that can reach people where they are—and on leaders who are equipped to invest across the full set of levers that build brain capital: prevention, care delivery, skills development, and measurement.

Brain capital (brain health + brain skills) can offer a strategic lens to guide inclusive growth, community resilience, and long-term well-being. This is a big part of the conversation we’re advancing at NatCon—and one we’ll continue to build on with upcoming MHI work, including a new playbook on scaling mental health task sharing (more to come this week!).

If you’re in town, we’d love to connect. Our MHI team will be contributing to several sessions over the next few days and engaging with partners across the ecosystem who are turning evidence into action.

Leah Pollack Rebecca Farley David Nora Bock Lindsey Naeyaert Tracy Nowski Danish M. Todd Khozein Drew Holzapfel Anjlee Joshi Laura Murray Andi Smith Charles Ingoglia Kana Enomoto Cheryl Healy

#NATCON #StrongBrainsStrongEconomies #BrainCapital #InvestInMentalHealth #CCBHC #TaskSharing
